content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: Jan. 13, 1977

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Ken Finney

Subject: Telephone conversation with Judge Timlin.

In a telephone conversation January 12, 1977, with Judge Timlin of the Corona Municipal Court I advised him that the money he had put into the Keogh Plan would not create any reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act. I told him that the appropriate way to treat the Keogh Plan funds was to look through the Keogh Plan to the uses to which he had put the funds. In his particular case, 80% of the funds were put into a savings account and 20% of the funds were put into the American Bar Association Retirement Plan (which in turn invests funds). Since neither of these uses of the funds amounted to an "investment" within the meaning of the Act, I advised the Judge that he had no reporting obligations. This was based on the fact that in neither situation did the Judge acquire any type of ownership interest in a business entity.

Dawn Wiser had spoken to the Judge earlier and I advised her of this disposition and told her to commit it to a memorandum.

KF:jo
